extra.reasoning: The three step events all reduce to acc := (3*acc + c) mod 8 (the triple-nested subtract-8 conditional is exactly mod-8 reduction since 3*acc+c <= 3*7+6 = 27 needs at most three subtractions). step_0 and step_2 use c=6, step_1 uses c=4 — both even. acc starts at 0 (even). Parity invariant: 3*acc has the same parity as acc, adding an even constant preserves parity, and reducing mod 8 (an even modulus) preserves parity. Hence acc is even on every reachable state. BFS/full enumeration over up to 8 step events confirms the reachable acc set is exactly {0,2,4,6}; acc==5 is never reachable. The only transition that sends after opt-out is t_resume_glitch, guarded by (opted_out and acc == 5). Since acc can never equal 5, that transition never fires; send_attempt after opt-out always takes t_send_blocked (sent := false). The optout transition itself sets sent := false. Therefore globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution within 10 events (and in fact unboundedly). No counterexample sequence exists; the target is unreachable.
